text: Excavated in 2021 by the means of archaeological rescue excavation in Prosiměřice 2 at the northern part of the cadastre in the area of the River Jevišovka floodplain, below the groundwater level. Similar to Prosiměřice 1, the Prosiměřice 2 hoard yielded nine metal ingots and parts of a broken sprue, two socketed axes. This points to the later period of the Middle Danube Urnfield Culture. All finds from the hoard were analyzed in terms of their chemical composition and proved to be made of relatively and different degrees of pure copper as well as tin bronze, which was used for the production of the axes, one ingot, and a sprue. Application of the X-ray computed tomography and wear traces analysis on the axes showed them to be to be imperfect, but in any case, fully functioning articles. In this respect, similar to Prosiměřice 1, the Prosiměřice 2 hoard yielded finds belonging to various stages of the metal production, and both hoards can be seen as parts of the area of a contemporary open settlement and the possible evidence of local metallurgical production. Similar evidence has also been observed in other findings of the metal hoards in the areas of the open settlements. Futhermore, due to their irrevocable deposition in the case of the Prosiměřice 2 as well, as well as the other multi-piece hoards in the water environment, we suppose them to be closely connected to communal socio-cultic rituals.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
